customer_message: Hi, I tried logging in this morning and keep getting a 403 error. My account is arcvault.io/user/jsmith. This started after your update last Tuesday.
normalized_text: Customer cannot log in and receives a 403 error. Account path: arcvault.io/user/jsmith. Issue began after last Tuesday's product update.

enrichment.core_issue: Customer login is failing with a 403 error after a recent product update.
enrichment.urgency_signal: User blocked from core login workflow.

summary: A customer reported that login is failing with a 403 error for account `arcvault.io/user/jsmith`. The issue likely relates to a recent product update and is routed to the Engineering queue for investigation.

customer_message: We'd love to see a bulk export feature for our audit logs. We're a compliance-heavy org and this would save us hours every month.
normalized_text: Customer requests a bulk export capability for audit logs to reduce manual compliance work.

enrichment.core_issue: Customer wants bulk export for audit logs to support recurring compliance workflows.
enrichment.urgency_signal: Time-saving request with recurring operational value, but no active service disruption.

summary: A customer asked for bulk export support for audit logs to reduce recurring compliance effort. This is a clear feature request with high model confidence, so it is routed to the Product queue at low priority.

customer_message: Invoice #8821 shows a charge of $1,240 but our contract rate is $980/month. Can someone look into this?
normalized_text: Customer reports invoice #8821 is billed at $1,240 instead of the contracted $980 monthly rate.

enrichment.core_issue: Customer believes invoice #8821 is overcharged relative to their contract rate.
enrichment.urgency_signal: Potential billing discrepancy with direct monetary impact.

summary: A customer flagged invoice `#8821` as billed at `$1,240` instead of their `$980` contract rate. The discrepancy is material enough for medium-priority billing review, but it stays out of escalation because the overcharge is below the `$500` threshold.

customer_message: I'm not sure if this is the right place to ask, but is there a way to set up SSO with Okta? We're evaluating switching our auth provider.
normalized_text: Customer asks whether ArcVault supports SSO setup with Okta while evaluating an authentication provider change.

enrichment.core_issue: Customer is seeking guidance on whether ArcVault supports SSO integration with Okta.
enrichment.urgency_signal: Pre-sales or implementation guidance request without service impact.

summary: A customer asked whether ArcVault can be configured for SSO with Okta as part of an auth-provider evaluation. This is routed to IT/Security as a low-priority technical question because there is no active outage or account issue.

customer_message: Your dashboard stopped loading for us around 2pm EST. Checked our end — it's definitely on yours. Multiple users affected.
normalized_text: Customer reports the dashboard stopped loading around 2pm EST and says multiple users are affected, with the issue likely on ArcVault's side.

enrichment.core_issue: Customer reports a likely ArcVault-side dashboard outage affecting multiple users.
enrichment.urgency_signal: Multiple users impacted by a probable service outage.

summary: A customer reported that the dashboard stopped loading around `2pm EST` and said multiple users are affected. Because the message strongly indicates an ArcVault-side outage, it is classified as a high-priority incident and sent directly to the Escalation Queue.

customer_message: I'm having trouble accessing some parts of the system after the update.
normalized_text: Customer reports vague access trouble affecting some parts of the system after a product update, without naming a specific feature, error, or affected scope.

enrichment.core_issue: Customer reports partial system access issues after an update, but the message is too ambiguous to classify more precisely.
enrichment.urgency_signal: Possible post-release issue, but the impact and failure mode are unclear from the message.

summary: A customer reported trouble accessing some parts of the system after an update, but did not provide enough detail to determine whether this is a bug, outage, or setup issue. It is classified conservatively as a technical question with low confidence and escalated for human review.
